persona.name: Rajesh Patel
persona.background: Watches every dollar and disputes nearly every fee. Will keep pushing for a waiver or a better rate well past the point most customers stop.

user_goals: Check the balance in his rental-income checking account before he reorganizes how rent comes in
Set up a recurring monthly transfer of $1,800 from rental checking into the property-reserve savings on the first of every month for the Maple Street unit
Set up a recurring monthly transfer of $2,100 from rental checking into the property-reserve savings on the first of every month for the Oak Avenue unit
Set up a recurring monthly transfer for the Birch Lane unit on the first as well — he first says $1,500 then corrects it to $1,650 partway through
Send a one-time $1,650 transfer from rental checking to property-reserve savings now because the Birch Lane tenant prepaid this month directly
Pull the fee history on the rental checking account to find a wire fee he thinks was charged in error
Submit a request to waive the $35 wire fee from June 4th, arguing it was the bank's processing mistake

tools.name: verify_customer_identity
tools.description: Verify customer identity using account number, SSN last 4, or security questions. Most account-accessing and state-changing tools require a successful identity verification first; call this before reading balances, transactions, or making any changes.

tools.name: get_account_balance
tools.description: Get current balance for a specific account. Requires verified identity. Resolve the customer-facing description (e.g. 'my checking account') to the correct account ID before calling.

tools.name: initiate_transfer
tools.description: Execute a one-time, immediate transfer of funds between two of the customer's own accounts. Requires verified identity. NOTE: this is a single transfer that happens once. For a repeating automatic schedule use setup_recurring_transfer instead — do not confuse the two.

tools.name: setup_recurring_transfer
tools.description: Set up a recurring automatic transfer on a schedule (daily/weekly/biweekly/monthly). Requires verified identity. This does NOT move money immediately; it schedules future transfers. For a single immediate move use initiate_transfer.

tools.name: get_fee_history
tools.description: Retrieve fees charged to an account, optionally filtered by fee type and look-back period. Requires verified identity. Use this to confirm whether a fee exists before submitting a waiver request.

tools.name: request_fee_waiver
tools.description: Submit a request to waive a specific fee. Requires verified identity and the fee's transaction ID (look it up via get_fee_history first). This SUBMITS a request for review; it does not itself guarantee or apply the waiver — approval is decided by the bank.

tools.name: get_interest_rates
tools.description: Get current interest rates for account and product types. This is public rate information and does NOT require identity verification. Optionally scope by customer segment.

initial_message: Hello, this is Rajesh. I've got three rental units and I'm tired of moving rent money around by hand every month, so I want to automate it. And while we're at it, there's a fee on my account I'm not happy about. Let's get into it.

expected_state_changes.goal: Set up a recurring monthly transfer of $2,100 from rental checking into the property-reserve savings on the first of every month for the Oak Avenue unit
Set up a recurring monthly transfer for the Birch Lane unit on the first as well — he first says $1,500 then corrects it to $1,650 partway through
Send a one-time $1,650 transfer from rental checking to property-reserve savings now because the Birch Lane tenant prepaid this month directly
Submit a request to waive the $35 wire fee from June 4th, arguing it was the bank's processing mistake
